summary: Handle the everyday Secrets Manager requests against already-provisioned secrets: tag and description edits (self-service), enabling rotation and re-tuning its cadence (guardrailed), disabling rotation with an evidence trail, and scheduling a secret for deletion (engineer-only, irreversible). No operation ever accepts a raw secret value — secret material is set and rotated out-of-band, so nothing here puts plaintext into Terraform state. "Rotate now" and "update value" are intentionally omitted: they are out-of-band API actions, not Terraform changes.

operations.title: Update governance tags on a secret
operations.summary: Add or update governance tags on this secret, like owner or cost center. No effect on its value or rotation.
operations.description: Add or edit the governance/billing tags (Application Name, PIC, Manager, CostCenter, etc.) on an existing secret. Metadata-only: does not touch the secret value, its KMS key, or rotation, and causes no interruption to readers.

params.name: secret
params.label: Target secret
params.help: The module-managed secret to retag, picked from inventory.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags to set
params.help: Key/value tags to add or overwrite. Existing keys not listed are left untouched.

operations.title: Edit a secret's description
operations.summary: Update this secret's description. Cosmetic only — no effect on its value, encryption, or rotation.
operations.description: Update the human-readable description on an existing secret. Cosmetic metadata change only — no effect on the secret value, encryption, rotation, or any reader.

params.name: secret
params.label: Target secret
params.help: The secret whose description to edit, picked from inventory.

params.name: description
params.label: New description
params.help: New description text (1-256 chars).

operations.title: Enable automatic rotation on a secret
operations.summary: Turn on automatic rotation for this secret. Applying rotates the credential immediately, which can briefly break apps that don't re-read it.
operations.description: Attach a new aws_secretsmanager_secret_rotation block to an existing secret, wiring a pre-provisioned engineer-owned rotation Lambda and a rotation schedule. Creating this resource triggers an immediate first rotation, so the live credential changes at apply time — any consumer that has not been wired to re-read the secret can see a brief authentication interruption.

params.name: secret
params.label: Secret to rotate
params.help: An existing secret that does not already have a rotation resource.

params.name: rotation_lambda_arn
params.label: Rotation function
params.help: A pre-provisioned, engineer-owned rotation function whose logic matches the target credential type, picked from this account's inventory. A rotation function not yet managed in Terraform must be adopted into the estate before it appears here.

params.name: automatically_after_days
params.label: Rotation interval (days)
params.help: How often the secret rotates, in days.

operations.title: Change rotation cadence on a rotating secret
operations.summary: Change how often this secret rotates. This only reschedules the next rotation — it doesn't change the value now.
operations.description: Adjust automatically_after_days on an existing rotation resource. This reschedules the next rotation; it does not force an immediate rotation and does not change the current credential, so readers are unaffected.

params.name: rotation
params.label: Target rotation
params.help: An existing rotation resource whose cadence to change.

params.name: new_automatically_after_days
params.label: New rotation interval (days)
params.help: New rotation interval in days; must differ from the current value.

operations.title: Disable rotation on a secret
operations.summary: Turn off automatic rotation for this secret. Its value stays readable, but it will no longer age out, so you must record why.
operations.description: Destroy the rotation resource so the secret stops rotating automatically. The secret and its current value remain fully readable, but the credential will no longer age out — this weakens the compliance posture, so a recorded justification is required.

params.name: rotation
params.label: Rotation to remove
params.help: An existing rotation resource to tear down.

params.name: reason
params.label: Justification
params.help: Why rotation is being disabled — recorded for the audit trail.

operations.title: Schedule a secret for deletion
operations.summary: Schedule this secret for deletion. It becomes unreadable right away, and once the recovery window passes, it's gone for good.
operations.description: Destroy the secret with a soft-delete recovery window. As soon as this applies the secret becomes unreadable — any application still calling GetSecretValue will fail authentication until the window is used to restore it or the value is re-created elsewhere. Irreversible past the recovery window; requires confirmation that no active readers remain.

params.name: secret
params.label: Secret to delete
params.help: The secret to schedule for deletion. Confirm no active readers via resource policy / access analyzer first.

params.name: recovery_window_in_days
params.label: Recovery window (days)
params.help: Soft-delete window during which the secret can still be restored.

params.name: confirm_no_active_readers
params.label: Confirm no active readers
params.help: Attestation that no live workload still reads this secret.

params.name: change_ticket
params.label: Change ticket reference
params.help: Approved change/decommission ticket ID for the audit trail.

operations.title: Provision a secret
operations.summary: Create the secret container (name, encryption, recovery window). The secret value is set out of band by an engineer.
operations.description: Create an aws_secretsmanager_secret: the METADATA container only (name, description, KMS encryption, recovery window). The secret value is NEVER captured here (no secret_string attribute is exposed) - an engineer sets it out of band after the secret exists. Submitting drafts the resource and routes it to an engineer to review and finish.

params.name: secret_name
params.label: Secret name
params.help: The name of the secret (written to the name attribute).

params.name: description
params.label: Description
params.help: A short note describing what the secret holds, never the value itself.

params.name: recovery_window_in_days
params.label: Recovery window (days)
params.help: How many days a deleted secret is recoverable before permanent deletion. A longer window protects against accidental loss.

params.name: kms_key_id
params.label: Encryption key
params.help: An optional customer-managed KMS key to encrypt the secret with (the aws_kms_key.arn it references); the AWS-managed key is used if left blank.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: Description
params.help: A short description recorded as the resource's Description tag.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: The team or person accountable for this resource, recorded as its PIC tag.

decisions: The secret VALUE is never captured here: this form provisions the secret container only. An engineer sets the value out of band after the secret exists, so no secret material ever passes through the portal.
Confirm the recovery window: a shorter window speeds permanent deletion, a longer window protects against accidental loss.
